macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Perek and Overview of Categorizing Mitzvot by Geography

micro_segments.title: Opening and Theme of the Perek: Categorizing Acquisitions and Mitzvot
Transition from Gender-Based Mitzvot to Land-Dependent Mitzvot

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Mitzvot Dependent on the Land of Israel

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Any Mitzvah Dependent on the Land Applies Only in Eretz Yisrael
Exceptions: Orlah and Kilayim Apply Even Outside Eretz Yisrael
Rabbi Eliezer: Chadash Also Applies Outside Eretz Yisrael

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Understanding 'Dependent on the Land' (Teluyah Ba'aretz)

micro_segments.title: Initial Proposal: Teluyah Means the Verse Mentions Entering the Land
Objection: Tefillin and Peter Chamor Say 'Entering' Yet Apply Everywhere
Rashi's Distinction: Not About Entering, But About the Nature of the Mitzvah

macro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's Resolution: Chovat Haguf vs. Chovat Karka

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda: Personal Obligations Apply Everywhere; Land-Related Ones Only in Eretz Yisrael
Source: Midrash on 'These Are the Laws' and 'All the Days You Live on the Land'
Teaching Paradigm: Destroying Avodah Zarah as Model for Chovat Haguf

macro_segments.title: The Chadash Debate: Rabbi Eliezer vs. Tanna Kamma

micro_segments.title: Tanna Kamma: Chadash Only in Eretz Yisrael; Meaning of Moshavoteichem as 'After Settlement'
Rabbi Eliezer: Chadash Applies Everywhere; Moshavoteichem Means 'Wherever You Dwell'

macro_segments.title: The Chadash Debate: Rabbi Eliezer vs. Tanna Kamma (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tanna Basis: Nesachim Only on Central Altar; Therefore Moshavoteichem Limited to After Settlement

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael vs. Rabbi Akiva: The Meaning of Moshavoteichem

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael's Principle: Moshavoteichem Combined with 'Coming' Means Only After Settlement
Rabbi Akiva's Objection: Shabbat Says Moshavoteichem But Applies Everywhere
Rabbi Yishmael's Response: Shabbat is Unique; The Combination Rule Applies to Chadash

macro_segments.title: Practical Halakha on Chadash: The Rishonim's Handling of a Forbidden Grain in Diaspora

micro_segments.title: Conflict Between Mishna in Orlah (Chadash Prohibited Everywhere) and Dispute in Kiddushin
Tosafot's Position: We Should Be Stringent on Chadash Outside Eretz Yisrael
Historical Practice: Using Rov (Majority) and Safek Sefeika to Permit Grains and Beer
Modern Considerations: Information Now Available About Grain Sources and Taking Root

macro_segments.title: The Orlah Problem: How We Drink Wine from Grafted Vines Outside Eretz Yisrael

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's Problem: Most Vines Are Grafted; Grafting Makes New Growth Orlah
Tosafot's Solution: Vines Are Layered (Mavrich), Not Grafted; Still Attached to Old Root
Source: Yerushalmi on Mavrich; When Attached, Still Has Status of Old Plant
Sign: When Still Attached to Old Vine, Leaves Face Away; When Severed, They Turn Back

macro_segments.title: Nesachim Debate: The Underlying Dispute Between Rabbi Yishmael and Rabbi Akiva

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael: Individuals Did Not Bring Nesachim in the Wilderness
Consequence: For Yishmael, Moshavoteichem Cannot Mean Individual Altars; Only After Settlement
Rabbi Akiva: Individuals Already Brought Nesachim in the Wilderness
Consequence: For Akiva, Moshavoteichem Must Mean Individual Altars; Wherever You Dwell

topical_tags.term: Rashi on Shema and geulah (exile return)
